subject: [POLICY] Enable apex-pdp to mount cert files
commit_message:
[POLICY] Enable apex-pdp to mount cert files

This changeset allows APEX-PDP charts to mount keysstore/truststore
files into the container during deployment.
Note: This is not for the ONAP common certificates, but to allow the
possibility to add custom certificates specific to deployments.
With this change, adding the required certificate files into
resources/certs folder will mount it into the respective location in the
container, which in turn can be used by apex-pdp to connect to different
systems specific to the setup.

Change-Id: Id5edcd998e34a17bf93c4e592fa45737c9e60d35
Issue-ID: POLICY-3232
Signed-off-by: a.sreekumar <ajith.sreekumar@bell.ca>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

We have the ability to have several specific certificates to be added to main trust store file (by giving a secret or a configmap holding these certificates) so I don’t think we need this here.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> We have the ability to have several specific certificates to be added to main trust store file (by giving a secret or a configmap holding these certificates) so I don’t think we need this here.

Okay! If the common charts already support mounting trustore/keystore jks files etc, then that is great.
I actually had a quick look before making this change, but couldn't find anything.
Could you please guide me on how to do that or point me to the right place to look in documentations? 
Mainly I have below questions:
- Where should I keep the truststore jks file during deployment, so that it gets mounted?
- The directory to which these files gets mounted has to be configurable. How to do this?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > We have the ability to have several specific certificates to be added to main trust store file (by giving a secret or a configmap holding these certificates) so I don’t think we need this here.
> 
> Okay! If the common charts already support mounting trustore/keystore jks files etc, then that is great.
> I actually had a quick look before making this change, but couldn't find anything.
> Could you please guide me on how to do that or point me to the right place to look in documentations? 
> Mainly I have below questions:
> - Where should I keep the truststore jks file during deployment, so that it gets mounted?
> - The directory to which these files gets mounted has to be configurable. How to do this?

Hi,
the best way is to create a config map.
So let's say you want ton onboard file.pem (pem files only are supported) for policy.
First you create the namespace where ONAP will be deployed:
kubectl create ns onap
then you create your configmap:
kubectl create configmap custom_certs -n onap --from-file=file.pem
then, we you install onap, you give the name of this configmap:
helm deploy onap local/onap --namespace onap --set global.masterPassword=myAwesomePasswordThatINeedToChange --set global.customCertsConfigMap=custom_certs

and it should be good

> > - The directory to which these files gets mounted has to be configurable. How to do this?
> 
> Hi,
> the best way is to create a config map.
> So let's say you want ton onboard file.pem (pem files only are supported) for policy.
> First you create the namespace where ONAP will be deployed:
> kubectl create ns onap
> then you create your configmap:
> kubectl create configmap custom_certs -n onap --from-file=file.pem
> then, we you install onap, you give the name of this configmap:
> helm deploy onap local/onap --namespace onap --set global.masterPassword=myAwesomePasswordThatINeedToChange --set global.customCertsConfigMap=custom_certs
> 
> and it should be good

Thanks @Sylvain Desbureaux. I was trying out your instructions from the channel.
I put my pem file into kubernetes/common/cert-wrapper/resources/ , then executed make command on common and policy , and then redeployed policy.
But after these steps, on checking the truststoreONAPall.jks, seems that the certificate is not added to it.

So I digged further, and tried to set 'importCustomCertsEnabled: true' and under certInitializer 'truststoreMountpath: /opt/app/policy/apex-pdp/etc/ssl/test', and tried the above steps.
But then, the init container 'policy-apex-pdp-import-custom-certs' is failing. Only available log message is "/root/import-custom-certs.sh: not found"

I also tried keeping the pem file under common/certInitializer/resources as found here: https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/common/certInitializer/templates/_certInitializer.yaml#L113
Still the same results.

Abandoned

With the fix done in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/121415 , OOM can already support mounting custom certificates. After testing with different scenarios, the findings are documented here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Mounting+custom+certificates+to+APEX-PDP+with+OOM+deployment
